title: Section — Project Identity / Project Bio
description: Everything needed to CREATE the project in any downstream system: who, what, where, how it is delivered and paid, the key dates, and the bonding/insurance/wage regime. For JOC/IDIQ work, the contract-vehicle and unit-price-book block is populated. Values are normalized but never invented; each carries provenance.

market_sector.description: e.g. commercial office, healthcare, education, federal, industrial, multifamily.

building.description: Physical facts useful for unit-rate and conceptual estimating.

construction_type.description: IBC construction type (e.g. II-B, V-A) when stated.

commercial_terms.description: Bonding, insurance, wage, and tax regime that drive estimate markups and eligibility.

tax_status.description: e.g. tax-exempt owner, sales tax on materials applies.

set_aside.description: Socioeconomic set-aside (e.g. SDVOSB, 8(a), small business) when applicable.

joc.description: Job Order Contracting / IDIQ specifics. Populated only when delivery_method is joc/idiq.

contract_vehicle.description: Master IDIQ/JOC contract id this task/job order falls under.

unit_price_book.description: Catalog the priced line items reference (e.g. RSMeans/Gordian, agency UPB) and its year/locality.

coefficient.description: Awarded adjustment factor applied to UPB unit prices (e.g. 0.92, 1.15).

coefficient_band.description: Which coefficient applies (e.g. normal hours, after-hours, secure facility) when multiple exist.

min_max_order.description: Minimum/maximum single order value when stated.

key_parties.description: Owner, A/E of record, GC/CM, program manager — references into the subcontractors/contacts directory by party_id.
